mixStrategy:
FREQUENCY SEPARATION: Bass (Electric Bass, GM 33) owns 20-300Hz with notes E1-E3 (MIDI 28-52), providing the rhythmic and harmonic foundation with driving eighth-note patterns that lock tightly with the kick drum. Chords (Warm Pad, GM 89) fill 300Hz-2kHz with sustained power chords (root+5th) in the E2-B4 range (MIDI 40-71), creating a thick harmonic bed underneath. Melody (Overdriven Guitar, GM 29) cuts through at 1-8kHz with pentatonic riffs in E4-E5 (MIDI 64-76), playing the lead voice with punchy rhythmic stabs and melodic phrases. Drums (GM 0 channel 10) provide full-spectrum rhythmic drive with kick on 1&3, snare on 2&4, hi-hat eighths, and crash accents at section boundaries.

HARMONIC PLAN: The progression is Em → D → C → D (i → bVII → bVI → bVII) for the verse segments, and Em → C → D → D (i → bVI → bVII → bVII) for the chorus/peak segments. All tracks change chords on the SAME beats. Each chord lasts 2 bars (4 beats = ~2.087s at 115 BPM).

RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK: Bass plays driving eighth notes that align with kick drum hits on beats 1 and 3. Snare accents beats 2 and 4 for backbeat drive. Hi-hat plays steady eighths for momentum. Melody riffs land on chord tones on strong beats and use pentatonic passing tones on weak beats. The pad sustains underneath to glue everything together.

ENERGY ARC (6 segments of ~7.5s each): Seg1 (0-7.5s) 0.4 energy — sparse intro, bass and drums only, lower velocities. Seg2 (7.5-15s) 0.6 — chords enter, melody hints. Seg3 (15-22.5s) 0.8 — full band, melody riffs intensify. Seg4 (22.5-30s) 0.9 — peak verse energy, dense riffing. Seg5 (30-37.5s) 1.0 — climax, highest velocities, crash cymbals, most dense. Seg6 (37.5-45s) 0.7 — controlled descent, sparser melody, sustained chords fade.
scale: E natural minor / E minor pentatonic / E blues scale
